gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Everything you need to start using MagellAIn on the water.

items.title: Creating Your Account
items.content: Sign up with your email, or use Google or Apple sign-in on the sign-in screen. Once in, go to Menu > My Boats to add your primary boat. MagellAIn uses your boat details to personalize AI coaching, performance analytics, and race data to your specific setup.

items.title: Adding Your Boat
items.content: Go to Menu > My Boats > Add Boat. Enter your boat's name, class (e.g. J/70, S2 7.9, Cal 25), sail number, and PHRF rating if applicable. MagellAIn uses this to tailor performance analytics and AI coaching to your specific boat. You can add multiple boats if you race on different vessels.

items.title: Your Home Dashboard
items.content: The home screen is your at-a-glance command center. It shows an AI-generated sailing conditions summary (rated Excellent, Good, Fair, Marginal, or Poor) with tips for the day, current wind and wave data from NOAA and NDBC buoys, your next scheduled race from FYC or WSSC, your primary boat details, quick-access buttons for Chart, Weather, Coach, and GPS Speed, and a live sailing news feed pulling recent articles from Sail Magazine, Scuttlebutt, World Sailing, and Sailing World. Sign in to also see your boat and personalized data.

items.title: Navigating the App
items.content: MagellAIn uses a bottom navigation bar designed for one-handed use on the water. The five main tabs are: Home (dashboard overview), Map (nautical charts, GPS tracking, race courses, and live buoy data), Weather (marine forecasts and wind data), Coach (AI sailing coach with voice input), and Menu (profile, boats, races, performance, crew messages, safety, settings, and help).

mapAndCharts.title: Map and Charts
mapAndCharts.description: Interactive nautical charts with live data overlays, GPS tracking, track replay, race courses, and tactical analysis.

items.title: Using the Map
items.content: The map view centers on your home waters (default: Ford Yacht Club, Lake Erie / Detroit River). Pinch to zoom, drag to pan. The map overlays nautical chart data showing depths, channel markers, buoys, and hazards. All data is specific to Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River. Multiple panels are available at the corners of the map: wind arrows and buoy data (upper area), course selection and GPS tools (lower area), race timer (lower right), and tactical analysis (lower left).

items.title: Pre-Race Start Timer
items.content: Tap the Flag icon in the lower-right of the map (above the GPS Tracker) to open the Start Timer panel. Tap '5 MIN' to begin the standard 5-minute US Sailing starting sequence — the timer counts down through Warning (yellow), Prep (orange), and One Minute (red) phases, then switches to a racing elapsed clock when the gun fires. Tap 'SYNC' instead when you hear the actual race committee gun — the timer immediately starts your racing clock from that moment. Audio signals play at each phase transition: three beeps at Warning and Prep, six rapid beeps at One Minute, and a long horn tone at the start. Tap Pause to freeze the countdown and resume with Play. Tap Reset to return to idle.

items.title: Wind Arrow Overlay
items.content: Toggle the wind arrow overlay from the map controls panel (upper-left of the map). When on, colored directional arrows appear at each NDBC buoy station across Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River, showing the current observed wind direction and speed. Arrow color indicates intensity: green for light air, yellow for moderate breeze, orange for fresh, and red for strong or gale-force winds. Arrow size grows with wind speed. Tap any arrow to see a popup with the station name, exact wind speed in knots, compass direction, and gust speed if available. Arrows update whenever the weather data refreshes.

items.title: Live Buoy Data
items.content: Tap any NDBC buoy marker on the map to see real-time observations: wind speed and direction, wave height and period, water temperature, and air pressure. Key stations for our area include Marblehead OH, South Bass Island OH, Toledo Harbor OH, and Dunkirk NY. Offshore Great Lakes buoys (45005, 45132, 45142) are seasonal and deploy in late spring. Data refreshes automatically every few minutes.

items.title: Race Course Selection
items.content: Tap the Race Courses button in the lower-right corner of the map (below the GPS Tracker) to open the course selection panel. It lists all active race courses from Ford YC and WSSC stored in the database, with course type (windward-leeward, triangle, etc.) and total distance in nautical miles. Tap any course to select it and see its legs — each leg shows the mark name, rounding direction (port or starboard), and order. Toggle 'Show course marks on map' to display all racing marks as colored pins on the chart. Select a course and tap 'AI Tactical Analysis' to get leg-by-leg tactical guidance based on current wind conditions.

items.title: AI Tactical Analysis
items.content: After selecting a race course in the Course Panel, tap the 'AI Tactical Analysis' button to open the tactical analysis panel. For each leg of the course it shows the leg bearing (true degrees and compass direction), leg distance in nautical miles, your point of sail (Close Hauled, Beam Reach, Broad Reach, Running, etc.) based on the current wind direction from NDBC buoys, and specific tactical advice tailored to conditions. For example: 'Hike hard, depower with traveler/backstay in 18 knots upwind' or 'Fly the spinnaker if available on the broad reach.' The analysis updates with live wind data. It does not draw visual laylines on the map — use it alongside the AI coach for deeper strategic questions.

items.title: GPS Track Recording
items.content: The GPS Tracker panel appears in the lower-right corner of the map (above the race timer). Tap 'Start' to begin recording your track — your device GPS will log position, speed, and heading continuously. Live stats show elapsed time, current speed in knots, total distance in nautical miles, and max speed. When finished, tap 'Stop' then 'Save' to store the track. Saved tracks sync to your account when signed in, or save locally if you are offline or not signed in.

items.title: Track Replay
items.content: Tap the Route icon in the lower-left corner of the map to open the Track Replay panel. It lists all your saved tracks with date, duration, distance, and max speed. Tap a track to load it — the full route draws on the map in orange. Use the playback controls to Play, Pause, or Stop the replay. A pulsing dot moves along the route showing your position at each recorded moment. Choose from 1x, 5x, 20x, or 50x playback speed. Tap the eject icon to return to the track list without clearing the map.

items.title: Speedometer and GPS Speed
items.content: The on-screen speedometer displays your current GPS boat speed and heading when location tracking is active. You can share your live speed and conditions with the AI coach by mentioning them in your conversation (e.g. 'I'm doing 5.2 knots upwind in 14 knots of breeze — any trim tips?').

items.title: Start Line Bias Tool
items.content: Tap the Flag button in the map controls panel (left side) to open the Start Line Bias tool. Place the two ends of your start line on the chart: first tap 'Boat / Pin End,' then tap the map where the boat end of the line is, then tap 'Committee Boat End' and tap the committee boat position on the chart. The tool calculates the bias angle — how many degrees the line is tipped from perpendicular to the current wind. A positive bias (green) means the port/pin end is favored; a negative bias means the starboard/committee end is favored. Bias under 2° is treated as a square line. The line itself draws on the chart in amber with a green dot at the boat end and blue dot at the committee end. Wind direction is pulled automatically from the nearest active NDBC buoy. Tap 'Clear Line' to reset and re-mark. This tool works best in the 5-15 minutes before your start, after the wind has settled. Reassess if the wind shifts significantly.

items.title: Wind Shift Graph
items.content: Tap the Trending Up button in the map controls panel to open the Wind Shift panel, which shows the last 90 minutes of wind direction changes from any active NDBC shore station. Use the dropdown to switch between stations. The graph plots wind direction over time, and the summary line tells you if the wind is veering (shifting clockwise — S to SW to W) or backing (shifting counter-clockwise). Veering wind in the northern hemisphere often indicates an approaching warm front. Backing wind can indicate increased pressure and stronger conditions ahead. This panel is also available on the Weather tab as 'Wind Shift History.' Data comes directly from the NDBC realtime observation feed, typically updated every 10-30 minutes depending on the station.

items.title: Layline Overlay
items.content: Enable laylines from the Layers panel (tap the Layers icon in map controls, then check 'Laylines'). Laylines appear as dashed lines extending from the windward mark — green for port tack, red for starboard tack. They show you exactly where to tack to fetch the mark on either tack. The tacking angle slider (30–55°, default 42°) lets you tune the calculation to your boat and conditions: a flat-water J/70 in 15 knots might tack in 40°, while a heavy-displacement PHRF boat in chop might be 48°. Laylines require a race course to be selected in the Course Panel and live wind data from an active NDBC station. They update automatically when wind direction changes. Note: laylines shown are theoretical — always account for tide, current, and pressure bands in your actual sailing.

items.title: Pre-Race Checklist
items.content: Tap the Checklist icon (list with lines) in the map controls panel to open the Pre-Race Checklist. The checklist has five sections: Rig (mast, rigging, running gear), Sails (halyards, battens, telltales, spinnaker), Safety (PFDs, horn, throwable, flares, VHF), Boat (bung, bilge, hatches, fire extinguisher), and Race Day (protest flag, class flag, timer sync, course number, crew brief, start plan). Tap any category header to expand it, then tap individual items to check them off. A progress bar at the top tracks your overall readiness. Tap 'Reset' to clear all checks before the next race, or 'Check All' to mark everything done when you know the boat is ready. Checklist state is session-only — it resets when you reload the app.

weather.title: Weather and Wind
weather.description: Detailed marine forecasts interpreted through a sailing lens.

items.title: Weather Dashboard
items.content: The Weather tab is in the bottom navigation bar (third from left). It shows a sailing conditions summary with a rating (Excellent through Poor) and tips, followed by current observations from all active NDBC stations in Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River. Wind data is displayed in knots with direction and gusts. A color-coded severity scale matches Beaufort categories. Tap Refresh to pull the latest data from NOAA.

items.title: Understanding Wind Data
items.content: Wind is displayed in knots with direction shown as both a compass bearing in degrees and a compass abbreviation (e.g. 'ESE'). Color-coded severity bands map to Beaufort scale categories — green for calm/light air, yellow for breeze, orange for fresh, red for strong/gale. Data is sourced from NDBC buoy observations at shore stations and offshore buoys. Offshore buoys (Station 45005 W Lake Erie, 45132, 45142) are seasonal and show 'Not yet deployed' in early spring — they typically deploy in May.

items.title: Weather Alerts
items.content: MagellAIn monitors active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area. Small Craft Advisories, Gale Warnings, and Thunderstorm Warnings appear as prominent amber banners on the Home and Weather screens. These alerts also feed into the AI coach, which can help you interpret conditions and decide whether to sail.

items.title: Wind Shift History
items.content: The Weather tab includes a Wind Shift History section below the buoy station cards. Select any shore station using the dropdown, then view a chart of wind direction over the last 90 minutes. A summary line indicates whether the wind is veering (shifting clockwise) or backing (shifting counter-clockwise) and by how many degrees. Tap Refresh to reload the latest NDBC observation data. The same wind shift chart is also accessible directly from the Map tab via the Trending Up button in the controls panel.

items.title: Station Observations
items.content: Each buoy station card shows wind speed and gust, wave height (where available), air temperature, water temperature, and barometric pressure. Active shore stations in our area include Marblehead OH (MRHO1), South Bass Island OH (SBIO1), Toledo Harbor OH (THRO1), and Dunkirk NY (DBLN6). Offshore seasonal buoys become available once deployed in spring.

aiCoach.title: AI Sailing Coach
aiCoach.description: Your personal Claude-powered sailing intelligence assistant with voice input and live conditions context.

items.title: Talking to Your Coach
items.content: The Coach tab connects you to MagellAIn's AI sailing coach, powered by Claude. Ask questions in plain English about racing tactics, sail trim, weather interpretation, rules, or boat handling. The coach knows your boat type, your waters, and current conditions, so answers are specific and actionable rather than generic.

items.title: Streaming Responses with Live Weather
items.content: The AI coach streams responses in real time — words appear as they are generated rather than arriving all at once after a delay. This means you start reading useful information within a second or two of sending a question. The coach also has access to live current conditions from the NDBC buoys when formulating a response, so if you ask what the wind is doing right now, it will reference actual observed readings from the nearest active station.

items.title: Voice Input
items.content: Tap the microphone button to the left of the text input to speak your question hands-free. A pulsing indicator shows when the coach is listening. Speak naturally — your words appear in the input field as you talk. When you stop speaking, the text is ready to review and send. Voice input requires microphone permission in your browser settings. Available on most modern mobile browsers.

items.title: What You Can Ask
items.content: The AI coach handles a wide range of sailing topics: pre-race strategy ('Wind is 8 knots out of the SW, which end of the line should I start?'), rules questions ('Boat on port tack crossed me at the windward mark, do I have rights?'), trim advice ('My J/70 is slow upwind in 12-15 knots, what should I check?'), weather interpretation ('Is this frontal passage going to shift the wind?'), post-race analysis ('I finished 4th — what should I work on?'), and course tactics ('We're sailing Course B today with a WSW wind, what's the plan?').

items.title: Context-Aware Coaching
items.content: The AI coach automatically factors in current weather conditions from your area, your boat's specifications and PHRF rating, and local knowledge about Lake Erie and Detroit River sailing patterns. The more boat and conditions information you share in the conversation, the more specific and useful the advice becomes. For tactical analysis on a specific course, use the map's AI Tactical Analysis panel first, then bring your questions to the coach.

items.title: Chat History
items.content: Your conversation with the AI coach is kept active while the Coach tab is open. Navigating away and returning within the same browser session will usually restore your messages, but a full page reload starts fresh. Tap the trash icon in the top-right corner of the Coach screen to clear the conversation and start a new one at any time.

racing.title: Race Management
racing.description: Schedules, results, performance analytics, and race result import for your racing.

items.title: Race Schedules
items.content: Access Races from Menu > Races or from the Home screen race card. It shows upcoming and past events from Ford Yacht Club, West Shore Sail Club, and DRYA series. Events include Wednesday Night at the Races (WNATR), weekend regattas, and championship series. Tap any event to expand and see race-by-race results and series standings.

items.title: Race Results and Standings
items.content: Tap any regatta to expand it. If results have been entered, you'll see each race row with finish date and fleet. Tap a race row to expand the full fleet result including boat names, sail numbers, positions, and elapsed/corrected times. Series standings are computed automatically from results within the regatta, showing points and place per fleet.

items.title: Importing Race Results from CSV
items.content: Tap the 'Import' button on the Races screen (top-right) to open the CSV import wizard. The four-step wizard guides you through: (1) Upload — drag and drop or select a CSV file exported from YachtScoring, PHRF-LE, or any spreadsheet. (2) Column Mapping — the app auto-detects your column headers and maps sail number, boat name, skipper, elapsed time, corrected time, and finish position. Review and adjust as needed. (3) Select Race — choose an existing regatta and race number, or create a new one. (4) Preview and Confirm — review all entries before saving. Results are saved to your account and immediately appear in the Performance screen. Download a template CSV directly from the import page.

items.title: Performance Analytics
items.content: Menu > Performance shows your full race history for your primary boat. Key stats at the top: total races, wins, podiums, and average finishing position. The season trend chart plots your position across races over time. Below that, Series Results show your standings in each active regatta series. The Race Log lists every race in reverse chronological order — tap any row to expand the full fleet result for that race. Tap the PDF button to export your complete race log and analytics.

items.title: Head-to-Head Competitor Records
items.content: The Performance screen includes a Head-to-Head section showing all competitors you've raced against in the same fleet. For each boat you'll see: number of shared races, your win/loss record, and the PHRF time correction advantage or disadvantage in seconds per mile. This helps you identify your closest rivals and understand where you win or lose on corrected time.

crewMessages.title: Crew Messages
crewMessages.description: Chat with your sailing team through crew groups and direct messages.

items.title: Crew Messaging Overview
items.content: Access Crew Messages from Menu > Crew Messages. The Messages screen has two tabs: Crews (group conversations tied to a boat or team) and Direct (one-on-one messages). You must be signed in to use messaging. All messages are saved to your account.

items.title: Creating a Crew Group
items.content: Tap 'Create First Crew' (or the + button) on the Crews tab to create a new crew group. Give it a name (e.g. 'Impetuous Crew 2026') and optionally associate it with one of your boats. Once created, share the group name with your crew so they can join. Use crew groups for pre-race briefings, weather updates, and debrief discussions.

items.title: Direct Messages
items.content: The Direct tab shows one-on-one conversations with other MagellAIn users. These are useful for skipper-to-skipper or owner-to-tactician communication outside of group channels.

safety.title: Safety Features
safety.description: Emergency tools and safety planning for on-water confidence.

items.title: Emergency Assist
items.content: The Emergency page is accessible from Menu > Emergency. It provides one-tap calling to 911 and direct numbers for USCG Sector Detroit (313-568-9564) and USCG Station Toledo (419-691-4048). Tap 'Get My Location' to display your GPS coordinates in large text for relaying to rescue services. The page also includes safety reminders about PFD use, cold water risk, and VHF Channel 16 monitoring.

items.title: SOS Text to Emergency Contacts
items.content: After getting your location, tap any saved emergency contact's name to open a pre-filled SMS on your phone with your GPS coordinates and a Google Maps link. Tap Add on the Emergency page to save personal emergency contacts (name, phone, relationship). Contacts sync to your account. You can also set a primary emergency contact in Menu > Profile.

items.title: Float Plans
items.content: File a float plan before heading out through Menu > Float Plan. Enter your departure location, destination, expected return time, and number of persons aboard. Your vessel details are pre-filled from your boat profile. Shore contact information can be added for someone who should alert authorities if you do not return on time. Active plans are saved and viewable in the Active Plans tab until you close them after returning to dock.

items.title: Weather Safety Alerts
items.content: Active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens whenever a Small Craft Advisory, Gale Warning, or Thunderstorm Warning is in effect. The AI coach also references active alerts in its responses so you can make informed go/no-go decisions.

offlineMode.title: Offline Mode
offlineMode.description: MagellAIn keeps working even when you lose connectivity on the water.

items.title: How Offline Mode Works
items.content: MagellAIn uses a service worker to cache app pages and recently loaded data so the app continues to function when you lose signal on the water. All five main screens (Home, Map, Weather, Coach, and Menu) remain accessible offline with cached content. An amber banner appears at the top of the screen automatically when your device goes offline, and disappears when connection is restored.

items.title: GPS Tracking Offline
items.content: GPS track recording works fully offline — your device's GPS does not need an internet connection. Tracks recorded while offline are saved to your device's local storage and will sync to your account automatically the next time you open the app with a connection.

items.title: What Data Is Available Offline
items.content: Weather data, race schedules, and AI coach conversations require an internet connection to refresh. However, the most recently loaded weather data, your saved race results, and your saved GPS tracks remain accessible from cache when offline. NOAA weather and buoy data is cached for up to one hour. Course data, performance records, and help content are also available offline once initially loaded.

items.title: Offline Page
items.content: If MagellAIn cannot load a page you have not visited before due to no connection, you will see the offline page with a prompt to return to home. Navigate to any recently visited screen and your cached data will be available.

settings.title: Settings and Preferences
settings.description: Customize MagellAIn to match how you sail.

items.title: Speed Units
items.content: Choose your preferred wind and boat speed units under Menu > Settings > Speed Units. Options include knots (standard nautical, recommended for racing), miles per hour, kilometers per hour, and nautical miles per hour (same as knots, displayed as nm/h). Knots is the default setting.

items.title: Display Theme
items.content: Switch between Light, Dark, and System themes under Menu > Settings > Appearance. Dark mode is recommended for use on the water as it reduces glare and improves readability in bright sunlight. The System setting automatically matches your device's current display mode.

items.title: Your Profile
items.content: Go to Menu > Profile to update your display name, home club, and emergency contact information. Your profile details help the AI coach calibrate responses to your experience level and local sailing area. Keeping your emergency contact current is important for the SOS feature.

items.title: Changing Your Password
items.content: If you signed up with email and password, you can change your password under Menu > Settings > Change Password. Enter your new password twice to confirm. If you signed in with Google or Apple, your password is managed by those accounts and cannot be changed here.
